title: [$250] Expose Accounting saved searches to Auditors

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
**Problem:** Currently in NewDot we only display Accounting saved searches to Admins. However, these are also useful reports for users with Auditor (i.e. read only) permission. Given these users can already see all the reports on the workspace they are an Auditor for, there is no reason they cannot also see the Accounting saved searches. **Solution:** Expose Accounting saved searches to Auditors cc @HaleyJacobson @kortneywynn @JmillsExpensify
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @mananjadhav

Created a workspace with default currency set to SGD 2. Navigated to Workflows > Add bank account 3. Attempted to set up a reimbursement bank account 4. Reached the “Choose your location” / country selection flow 5. Observed that: Singapore is not listed in the guided “Choose your location” options In some views, Singapore appears in dropdown, but the flow does not proceed correctly Previously encountered “Workspace currency not supported” message despite workspace being set to SGD ## Expected Result: Singapore should be available as a selectable option for Global Reimbursement User should be able to proceed with Singapore onboarding flow (DocuSign, verification, etc.) No “currency not supported” message should appear for SGD workspace ## Actual Result: Singapore is missing from the main Global Reimbursement setup flow Inconsistent behavior across UI - they have Singapore as an option under Classic SGD shown as supported in messaging Singapore not properly supported in flow User is blocked from completing bank account setup ## Workaround: Can the user still use Expensify without this being fixed? body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.64-0 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** No **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/87705 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** sdsodijnosdjoij@gmail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Exploratory - Significant User Experience Deterioration **Device used:** Mac 26.2 / Chrome **App Component:** Money Requests ## Action Performed: Precondition: - Enable Rules in workspace settings. - Enable Attendee tracking in Rules. 1. Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace chat. 3. Click + > Create expense > Manual. 4. Enter amount > Next. 5. On confirm page, click Attendees. 6. Add "banana", then add "apple". 7. Click Create expense. 8. Create one more expense (no need add attendee). 9. Open the report. 10. Bulk select both expenses. 11. Click dropdown button > Merge. 12. Select the 3 attendees in Attendees section, then select any for other sections. 13. Click Continue. 14. On confirm page, hover over Attendees field and click Copy button. 15. Close the RHP. 16. Paste the content. ## Expected Result: Attendees will be copied in alphabetical order because they are arranged alphabetically on confirm page. ## Actual Result: Attendees are not copied in alphabetical order when they are arranged alphabetically on confirm page. Copied attendees - banana, apple Attendees on confirm page - apple, banana ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ac632afd-1bef-4b0a-8722-7cc8cdb27c1a [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Require a magic code when provisioning a new company card

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Coming from a discussion in [this PR](https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/88345#discussion_r3148993082). # Problem A bad actor can work to gain control of an account from an unsuspecting user by adding the attacker's contact method as a secondary contact method during the company card provisioning flow (it's in the `WorkspaceCompanyCardAddWorkEmailPage` component), then setting that to the primary contact method (to receive future magic codes). ### Solution - Prompt the user for a magic code (we have this same flow in many different places in the app, so you can use those for inspiration) - Send that code to the API for setting the new default contact method - It's fine to assume for now that these requests will always be successful, even if an invalid code is entered. Eventually, the backend will start verifying the codes and return an error message if it isn't successful cc @rafecolton @shubham1286agra
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @shubham1206agra
